original_image_tags: A hand-drawn map of Japan showing several locations marked as observing stations (●). A line (—) labeled "PROBABLE PATH OF PHENOMENON" connects several of these stations. A red cross (✕) labeled "PROBABLE AREA OF EXPLOSION OR DISAPPEARANCE" is marked near one station. The observing stations are labeled: FUKUSHIMA, NAGANO, TAKAYAMA, SUWA, IIDA, and GIFU. The map includes a grid overlay. In the bottom left corner, there is a handwritten annotation: "Incl 4³".

elements.description: Hand-drawn outline map of Japan showing several locations marked with black dots labeled FUKUSHIMA, NAGANO, TAKAYAMA, SUWA, IIDA, and GIFU. A red 'X' is drawn near Gifu. A straight brown line connects Fukushima to the area near the red 'X'. A legend in the bottom right corner defines symbols: a black dot as 'OBSERVING STATION', a straight line as 'PROBABLE PATH OF PHENOMENON', and a red 'X' as 'PROBABLE AREA OF EXPLOSION OR DISAPPEARANCE'. Text 'Incl 4' with a superscript '2' is visible in the bottom left corner.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual elements: a hand-drawn map of Japan with six observing stations, a path line, a red X near Gifu, and a bottom-left annotation; the main difference is that Description B adds a legend in the bottom right corner that Description A omits, but they do not contradict each other.
